body: Mogo Kenya today announced a distribution partnership with OPPO Kenya that will place certified OPPO smartphones into the hands of more than 20,000 Kenyans over the next twelve months. Under the agreement, customers can walk into any of Mogo's 84 branches, select an eligible OPPO A-series or Reno-series device and leave the same day with a handset financed from KES 50 per day on M-Pesa.

The partnership is part of Mogo's Smartphone Loan programme, launched in late 2025, which targets the segment of working Kenyans who have a steady daily income but cannot afford the upfront cost of a new device. Eligibility is based on M-Pesa transaction history and a short in-branch interview — no CRB check is required.

"A smartphone is no longer optional. For a rider, it is the difference between one platform and three. For a shopkeeper, it is the difference between cash-only and digital payments," said the Mogo Kenya Head of Retail Finance. "Partnering with OPPO means customers get a certified device, a manufacturer warranty and a financing plan they can service on the same phone."

OPPO Kenya will supply handsets directly to Mogo branches, cutting out intermediary mark-ups. All devices ship with a 24-month manufacturer warranty and a 7-day return window. Customers who complete their loan on time qualify for an upgrade path to a higher-tier device at the end of the term.
The programme goes live across all 84 Mogo branches on 1 May 2026.
articles.dek: A new distribution agreement brings certified OPPO handsets within reach of boda riders, shopkeepers and first-time smartphone buyers.
articles.title: Valerie

body: Fresh off its OPPO agreement, Mogo Kenya has added Tecno Mobile to its Smartphone Loan panel, giving customers a second certified manufacturer to choose from at the point of sale. The Camon and Spark ranges will be available across all 84 Mogo branches from mid-April 2026.

Application volumes for Mogo's Smartphone Loan product have tripled since January, with single-branch days at the Nairobi CBD and Kisumu locations regularly clearing 40 applications. The addition of Tecno widens the price band on offer, starting at KES 38 per day on M-Pesa for entry-level handsets.

"Demand has been louder than we expected, especially from the boda and tuk-tuk segment," said the Mogo Kenya Retail Director. "A second manufacturer partner lets us keep stock moving and keeps the customer in control of the choice."

As with the OPPO agreement, all Tecno devices sold through Mogo come with a 24-month manufacturer warranty and a 7-day return window. Customers can apply in-branch with only a national ID and an active M-Pesa number.
articles.dek: Second manufacturer partnership in a month signals the speed at which device financing is reshaping Mogo's retail mix.
articles.title: Tecno joins Mogo Smartphone Loan line-up as demand outpaces supply

body: Mogo Kenya has crossed the 84-branch mark with the opening of its newest office in Lodwar, Turkana County. The milestone comes eighteen months after Mogo passed 60 branches and represents the company's most aggressive physical expansion since entering the Kenyan market.

The new branches are concentrated in counties that have historically been under-served by formal credit: Turkana, Marsabit, Tana River, West Pokot and Narok. Each branch is fully staffed with loan officers who speak the local languages, and each is within walking distance of at least one major market or matatu stage.

"We do not believe financing works unless it is local," said the Mogo Kenya Chief Executive. "A customer who can walk into a branch, sit opposite a loan officer and sign their contract in person is a customer who understands what they have signed. That is the model we are scaling."

Mogo's branch network now covers 38 of Kenya's 47 counties. The company has committed to opening at least eight further branches in 2026, with the remaining nine counties prioritised for the following financial year.
articles.dek: Eighteen new branches opened in the last twelve months, deepening Mogo's footprint from Lodwar to Lamu.
articles.title: Mogo Kenya reaches 84 branches nationwide

body: Mogo Kenya has disbursed more than KES 15 billion to Kenyan customers since it issued its first loan in 2018. The cumulative figure, audited through end of February 2026, places Mogo among the largest non-bank lenders to the informal-sector economy in East Africa.

Roughly 58 per cent of the total has gone into two-wheeler and three-wheeler asset finance — the boda-boda and tuk-tuk loans that remain Mogo's flagship retail product. Car logbook loans account for a further 29 per cent, with the balance split between check-off, MSME and smartphone financing.

"Fifteen billion shillings is not a vanity number. It is a measure of how much working capital Kenya's self-employed sector has been able to put to work," the Chief Executive said. "More than 70 per cent of that money has gone to customers who do not earn a formal salary."

The company expects to cross KES 20 billion in cumulative disbursements before the end of 2026, driven largely by electric-boda financing and the continued rollout of check-off loans to private-sector employers.
articles.dek: Cumulative lending passes a new threshold, with boda-boda and tuk-tuk financing still the largest single contributor.
articles.title: Mogo Kenya crosses KES 15 billion disbursed since launch

body: Mogo Kenya has launched the second phase of its electric boda-boda financing programme, adding a battery-swap facility to loans issued in Nairobi and Thika. Riders on the new product can swap a depleted battery for a fully-charged one at 14 partner stations for a fixed per-swap fee — eliminating the 90-minute charge window that has slowed adoption of electric two-wheelers in Kenya.

Phase one of the programme, launched in August 2025, financed more than 2,400 e-bodas at a rate of KES 451 per day on M-Pesa. Rider feedback was overwhelmingly positive on running costs but flagged charging time and range anxiety as the two largest operational concerns.

The new swap-station network has been built in partnership with two Kenyan assemblers, with stations co-located at major matatu stages and fuel forecourts. Phase two is expected to finance a further 5,000 e-bodas in 2026, with Kisumu and Mombasa scheduled to come online before the end of the third quarter.

Mogo's Head of Green Finance said the programme sits at the intersection of the company's two core commitments — accessible credit and a measurable environmental dividend — and that every financed e-boda is estimated to displace 1.6 tonnes of CO₂ per year compared with a petrol equivalent.
articles.dek: Mogo extends its e-boda programme with a swap-station pilot in Nairobi and Thika, addressing the last major barrier to rider adoption.
articles.title: Electric boda financing enters phase two with battery-swap pilot

body: Boxer Motors Kenya has named Mogo Kenya its preferred financing partner for the 2026 sales year, consolidating a relationship that began informally in 2022. Under the new agreement, every Boxer dealership in Kenya will operate a dedicated Mogo desk, and in-dealership applications will be eligible for a fast-track approval lane with decisions inside 30 minutes.

The partnership covers the full Boxer line-up, from the BM100 commuter to the larger 150cc models favoured by long-distance riders. Customers will be able to complete their purchase, financing and insurance in a single visit.

"Our customers want to ride out the same day they walk in. That is what this agreement guarantees," the Boxer Motors Kenya Country Manager said. "Mogo has the branch network, the product design and the turnaround time to make that promise credible."

The two companies have also committed to a joint rider-training programme that will offer free defensive-riding and road-safety sessions to every customer financed under the agreement. The programme is scheduled to launch at six pilot branches before rolling out nationwide.
articles.dek: Agreement guarantees Mogo pricing at Boxer dealerships nationwide and introduces a fast-track approval lane for in-dealership applications.
articles.title: Boxer Motors names Mogo Kenya preferred financing partner for 2026

body: Mogo Kenya has received formal renewal of its Digital Credit Provider licence from the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK), the national financial regulator. The renewal follows the routine two-year compliance review and covers the full range of Mogo's consumer lending activity, including logbook loans, smartphone financing and check-off products.

The review examined Mogo's pricing transparency, complaints handling, customer-data protection, AML controls and branch-level sales practices. The regulator confirmed that no material findings were raised against the company.

"Operating under a CBK licence is not a marketing line. It is a condition of doing business, and it is the reason customers can sit opposite one of our officers and know that the rate, the fees and the recovery terms they are being quoted are the ones written in the contract," said the Mogo Kenya Chief Compliance Officer.
Mogo was among the first cohort of lenders granted a DCP licence when the framework was introduced in 2022.
articles.dek: The Central Bank of Kenya has renewed Mogo's DCP licence for a further two-year term following a full compliance review.
articles.title: Mogo Kenya renews Digital Credit Provider licence with Central Bank

body: Mogo Kenya has crossed 50,000 active MSME customers, a segment the company only formally launched into in late 2023. The MSME loan offers working capital of up to KES 500,000 against business records and, where available, a logbook or household-asset collateral.

The customer profile skews toward businesses that have been operating for at least twelve months but do not qualify for traditional bank credit: salons, welding workshops, mitumba retailers, small agri-processors, tuk-tuk owner-operators and cyber cafés. Average ticket size is KES 180,000 against an average term of 14 months.

"The formal banking system has not solved for this customer. She earns real money, she has real assets, but she does not have audited accounts. We have built a product that looks at what she actually has, not what a spreadsheet says she should have," the Mogo Kenya Head of MSME said.
Mogo plans to expand the MSME team by a further 60 loan officers in 2026 and to introduce a secured overdraft facility later in the year.
articles.dek: Shopkeepers, salon owners, mechanics and farmers form the bulk of a fast-growing small-business book.
articles.title: 50,000 MSME customers now financed by Mogo Kenya

body: Mogo Kenya has distributed 12,000 certified motorcycle helmets and 8,000 reflective vests to boda-boda riders across 40 of its branches during December 2025. The drive, run in partnership with the National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA), is the single largest private-sector safety-gear distribution of the year.

Every customer financed by Mogo for a new boda-boda in the second half of 2025 received a helmet and vest free of charge. Walk-in riders at participating branches were also eligible to collect a helmet at no cost, subject to a short road-safety briefing delivered by Mogo and NTSA officers.

"A helmet is the cheapest thing standing between a rider and a closed-casket funeral," said the Mogo Kenya Head of Community. "If we are going to finance the bike, we should be the ones making sure the helmet is on it."
The programme will be repeated in the second quarter of 2026, with the target increased to 20,000 helmets.
articles.dek: Mogo Kenya closes 2025 with its largest-ever helmet and reflective-vest distribution across 40 branches.
articles.title: 12,000 helmets distributed in December road-safety drive

body: When Grace Moraa walked into Mogo's Kisii branch in 2019, she was renting a single chair at the back of a busy salon and saving to open her own. Her first Mogo loan was KES 85,000, secured against the logbook of her second-hand Toyota. She used it to lease a two-chair space and buy her first professional equipment.

Seven years, four loans and zero missed payments later, Grace has just opened her third branch — a nine-chair salon-and-spa on the Kisii–Kisumu road with eleven employees on payroll. Every step of the expansion has been financed through Mogo, with successive loans underwritten against her own business history rather than fresh collateral.
"The bank wanted audited accounts. Mogo wanted to sit in my salon, look at the diary and understand how I ran it," Grace said at the branch opening. "That is the difference."

Grace's file is the kind Mogo points to when it argues that relationship-based lending to micro-entrepreneurs is not only socially valuable but commercially sound. Customers who repay a first Mogo loan on time qualify for a pre-approved second facility at a reduced rate — a graduation path the company says has retained more than 60 per cent of its MSME customers across multiple cycles.
articles.dek: Grace Moraa opened a one-chair salon in Kisii in 2019. This month she cut the ribbon on her third branch — each one financed by Mogo.
articles.title: From salon chair to three-branch business: Grace's seven-year Mogo journey

body: Mogo Kenya has opened a new regional hub in Eldoret, Uasin Gishu County, consolidating the operational back-office for eleven branches across Western Kenya and the North Rift. The 1,200 square-metre facility houses loan origination, collections, training and a dedicated MSME underwriting team.

The hub model is the first of its kind for Mogo Kenya. Branch-level operations remain customer-facing, while back-office functions previously centralised in Nairobi are now devolved to the region, shortening turnaround times and putting underwriting decisions closer to the local market.

"Loan officers in Eldoret understand Eldoret. Loan officers in Nairobi do not always," the Mogo Kenya Regional Director said. "Moving the underwriting closer to the customer is not a cost-saving exercise — it is how you build a credit book that actually performs."
Mogo plans to open a second regional hub in Mombasa before the end of 2026, serving the Coast region. A third hub, covering the Lower Eastern counties, is under evaluation.
articles.dek: The new 1,200 square-metre facility consolidates loan origination, recovery and training for the eleven Western and North-Rift branches.
articles.title: Mogo opens Western Kenya regional hub in Eldoret

body: Mogo Kenya has launched a Smartphone Loan product that allows customers to walk out of any Mogo branch with a new handset for as little as KES 50 per day on M-Pesa. The product extends the company's established asset-finance model from boda-bodas, tuk-tuks and cars into consumer electronics.

Eligibility is based on a review of M-Pesa transaction history over the previous three months, a valid national ID and a short in-branch interview. Customers do not need a CRB score, a bank account or a payslip.

"A smartphone is no longer a luxury — it is the interface between a rider and his next job, between a shopkeeper and her supplier, between a farmer and the weather forecast," the Mogo Kenya Chief Product Officer said.
Handsets ship with a 24-month manufacturer warranty and a 7-day return window. Full terms, including total cost of credit, are printed on the in-branch offer sheet before the customer signs.
articles.dek: New product extends Mogo's asset-finance model from vehicles to devices, targeting the gap between pre-paid feature phones and full-price smartphones.
articles.title: Mogo Kenya launches Smartphone Loans from KES 50 per day

body: Mogo Kenya has opened its 66th branch in the town of Awendo, Migori County. The new office is positioned to serve the sugarcane-farming catchment around the South Nyanza Sugar Company, as well as the boda-boda and matatu operators plying the Awendo–Migori and Awendo–Rongo routes.

The branch will offer Mogo's full retail line-up: boda-boda and tuk-tuk financing, car logbook loans, MSME working capital and smartphone loans. Two loan officers fluent in Dholuo and Swahili have been assigned to the branch.

"Awendo is a classic Mogo market — a town that generates real cash flow but has historically been poorly served by formal credit," the Mogo Kenya Regional Director said. "Our job is to put a loan officer within walking distance of every customer who has earned one."

The Awendo opening takes Mogo into its sixth region of Kenya and brings the company a step closer to its stated target of eighty branches nationwide by the end of 2026 — a target that has since been exceeded.
articles.dek: The newest branch takes Mogo deeper into Nyanza, serving sugarcane farmers, boda riders and small traders along the Awendo corridor.
articles.title: Mogo opens 66th branch in Awendo, Migori County

body: Mogo Kenya has completed the first annual cycle of its financial-literacy curriculum in Kenyan secondary schools, reaching 45,000 Form 3 and Form 4 students across 120 participating schools in eight counties. The ten-week programme covers personal budgeting, the real cost of credit, mobile-money hygiene, fraud awareness and an introduction to asset financing.

The curriculum was developed in partnership with two Kenyan education NGOs and is delivered by trained Mogo volunteers during life-skills lesson blocks. The materials are provided free of charge to participating schools and carry no Mogo product-marketing content.

"We make our money extending credit. That is exactly why it is in our interest for every young Kenyan to understand how credit works before they take their first loan," the Mogo Kenya Head of Community said.
The programme will expand to 300 schools across twelve counties in the 2026 cycle.
articles.dek: Free ten-week programme reaches 45,000 students across eight counties in its first year.
articles.title: Mogo rolls out financial-literacy curriculum to 120 secondary schools

body: Eleving Group, the Nasdaq-listed parent of Mogo Kenya, has announced that its worldwide customer base has crossed one million active borrowers. The group, headquartered in Riga, Latvia, operates across 17 countries in Europe, Asia and Africa under the Mogo, Primero and ExpressCredit brands.

Mogo Kenya is one of the group's largest single-country operations by customer count. More than 100,000 active Kenyan customers now contribute to the group book, primarily through two-wheeler, three-wheeler and car financing.

"Kenya has moved from a growth market to a core market for this group," the Eleving Group Chief Executive said. "The product design, the branch network and the digital origination built in Kenya are already being adapted for deployment in our newer Africa operations."

The group has reiterated its strategic focus on the mobility-finance and productive-asset segments in emerging markets, and has named Sub-Saharan Africa as its primary region for organic expansion over the next three years.
articles.dek: Mogo Kenya's 100,000-plus customers form a growing share of a parent-group base that now spans 17 countries.
articles.title: Eleving Group crosses one million customers globally

body: Honda Kenya and Mogo Kenya have extended their in-dealership financing agreement to 22 Honda outlets nationwide, up from eight under the pilot agreement signed in early 2025. The expanded programme places a Mogo loan officer on site at each participating dealership, offering 30-minute approvals for new and certified pre-owned Honda motorcycles.

The programme covers the full Honda motorcycle line-up in Kenya, with most applications falling on the CB125 and CB150 commuter models favoured by boda-boda operators. Honda-certified pre-owned machines are also eligible.
"Honda customers ask two questions: how much deposit and how long to approve. In-dealership Mogo financing answers both on the showroom floor," the Honda Kenya Commercial Director said.
Mogo and Honda have also agreed a joint training programme that will deliver six rider-safety sessions per year at each participating dealership.
articles.dek: Fast-track in-dealership approvals now available at Honda showrooms from Nairobi to Kisii.
articles.title: Honda and Mogo expand dealer-floor financing to 22 Kenyan outlets

body: Mogo Kenya has extended its check-off loan facility beyond the Government of Kenya and Teachers Service Commission payrolls to cover private-sector employees at participating employers. The product offers unsecured personal loans of up to KES 500,000 with repayment collected directly at source from the borrower's monthly salary.

Disbursement is targeted inside 24 hours from application and does not require a CRB check. Employers onboard to the programme through a short MoU that grants Mogo a deduction instruction from the employee, subject to the Employment Act ceiling of two-thirds net pay.

"Check-off is the cleanest form of consumer credit there is," the Mogo Kenya Head of Salary Finance said. "The customer gets a fair rate. The employer carries no balance-sheet risk. And recovery is invisible to the customer's daily cash flow."
Thirty-one employers are live on the programme at launch, covering industries from horticulture to manufacturing to business process outsourcing. A further seventy employers are in onboarding.
articles.dek: Mogo extends its check-off facility beyond GoK and TSC employees, with disbursement inside 24 hours and no CRB check.
articles.title: Check-off loans extended to private-sector employees

body: Mogo Kenya's Rider Clinic — a travelling programme of free bike servicing, safety-gear distribution and financial-literacy briefings — completed its seven-stop Coast tour this week. More than 2,800 boda-boda riders attended across stops in Mariakani, Mtwapa, Kilifi, Malindi, Ukunda, Voi and Mombasa CBD.

Participating riders received a free safety inspection, a basic service on consumables such as brake pads and chain oil, and a take-home kit containing a helmet, reflective vest and tyre-pressure gauge. Mogo loan officers were on site at each stop to run voluntary one-to-one budgeting sessions.

"We are not running a marketing roadshow. We are running a safety programme that happens to have our branding on it," the Mogo Kenya Head of Community said. "Every rider in that queue has a family waiting at the end of the shift. Our job is to help make sure they get home."
The Rider Clinic will tour the Western and Nyanza regions in the second half of 2026.
articles.dek: Free bike servicing, safety-gear giveaways and financial-literacy sessions at seven stops between Mariakani and Malindi.
articles.title: Mogo Kenya's Rider Clinic tours the Coast region

body: TVS Motor Kenya and Mogo Kenya have signed a three-year preferred-financier agreement covering 34 TVS outlets nationwide. The agreement formalises a retail partnership that has been running informally since 2020 and names Mogo as the exclusive in-dealership retail financier for the TVS Apache, HLX and King three-wheeler ranges in Kenya.

Under the agreement, each TVS outlet will host a Mogo loan officer during standard showroom hours, and customers choosing in-dealership financing will be offered a bundled price that includes the machine, comprehensive insurance and registration fees.

"This agreement rewards a partnership that has already served thousands of Kenyan riders," the TVS Kenya Managing Director said. "Formalising it means our customers get the same product, the same rate and the same turnaround time at every one of our 34 outlets."
TVS-financed machines now account for a significant share of Mogo's two-wheeler and three-wheeler originations in Kenya.
articles.dek: Long-standing retail partnership formalised into a three-year exclusive arrangement across 34 TVS outlets.
articles.title: TVS Kenya and Mogo sign three-year preferred-financier agreement

body: Bajaj Auto and Mogo Kenya have launched a purpose-built financing programme for the Bajaj RE three-wheeler range, widely known locally as the tuk-tuk. The product offers daily M-Pesa repayments tailored to the cash-flow profile of owner-operators working the short-route taxi trade in Mombasa, Malindi and parts of Nairobi Eastlands.

The programme finances new and certified pre-owned Bajaj RE units against a deposit of 20 per cent, with an average term of 24 months. Repayments are collected daily on M-Pesa, in line with the way tuk-tuk operators already manage their earnings.

"The tuk-tuk operator is not on a monthly salary. His income arrives trip by trip, which is why a monthly instalment never fit him," the Bajaj Kenya Country Manager said. "A daily M-Pesa deduction works with his cash flow rather than against it."
Mogo has reported a low default rate on its existing three-wheeler book and expects the Bajaj programme to reach 1,500 financed units inside twelve months.
articles.dek: Purpose-built product targets owner-operators in Mombasa, Malindi and Nairobi Eastlands.
articles.title: Bajaj and Mogo launch dedicated tuk-tuk financing programme
